name: slupy

metadata.name: slupy
metadata.summary: Library that serves a variety of utility/helper functionality (in Python) built on the standard library.

documentation: Library that serves a variety of utility/helper functionality (in Python) built on the standard library. Our repository: https://github.com/Nishant173/slupy

functions.name: slupy.randomizer.randomizer.generate_random_hex_code
documentation: Generates random 6-digit hexadecimal code

functions.name: slupy.randomizer.randomizer.generate_random_hex_codes
documentation: Returns list of random 6-digit hexadecimal codes

functions.name: slupy.mathematics.mathematics.is_prime
documentation: Returns True if the given number is prime; otherwise returns False

functions.name: slupy.mathematics.mathematics.cumulative_aggregate
documentation:
Returns list of cumulative aggregates.
Options for `method` are: `["sum", "difference", "product", "division"]`.

functions.name: slupy.data_wrangler.utils.cmp
documentation:
Replacement for built-in function cmp that was removed in Python 3.

Compare the two objects x and y and return an integer according to
the outcome. The return value is negative if x < y, zero if x == y
and strictly positive if x > y.

Reference: https://portingguide.readthedocs.io/en/latest/comparisons.html#the-cmp-function

functions.name: slupy.data_wrangler.utils.multi_key_sort
documentation: Returns a new list with the sorted iterable

functions.name: slupy.data_wrangler.utils.drop_indices
documentation: Drops items at the given `indices` (in-place). Accepts only non-negative indices.

functions.name: slupy.data_wrangler.utils.keep_indices
documentation: Keeps items at the given `indices` (in-place). Accepts only non-negative indices.

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.get_timetaken_dictionary
documentation:
Returns dictionary having the keys: ['weeks', 'days', 'hours', 'minutes', 'seconds', 'milliseconds'] containing the time elapsed.

```pycon
>>> get_timetaken_dictionary(num_seconds=3725.4292)
>>> get_timetaken_dictionary(num_seconds=885354.128129)
```

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.get_timetaken_fstring
documentation: Returns f-string containing the elapsed time

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.is_first_day_of_month
documentation: Checks if the given date/datetime object represents the first day of any month

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.is_last_day_of_month
documentation: Checks if the given date/datetime object represents the last day of any month

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.is_first_day_of_year
documentation: Checks if the given date/datetime object represents the first day of any year

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.is_last_day_of_year
documentation: Checks if the given date/datetime object represents the last day of any year

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.get_first_day_of_current_month
documentation: Returns a new date/datetime object having the first day of the current month

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.get_last_day_of_current_month
documentation: Returns a new date/datetime object having the last day of the current month

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.get_first_day_of_next_month
documentation: Returns a new date/datetime object having the first day of the next month

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.is_february_29th
documentation: Checks if the given date/datetime object represents February 29th (of any year)

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.get_day_of_month_suffix
documentation: Returns the suffix of the given `day_of_month`

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.get_small_and_big_dates
documentation:
Returns tuple having `(small_date, big_date)` after comparing `x` and `y`. Ensures that `small_date` <= `big_date`.
Returns new instances of the date objects.

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.compare_day_and_month
documentation:
Compares only the day and month of the given date objects.
    - If a < b, returns '<'
    - If a > b, returns '>'
    - If a == b, returns '=='

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.update_year
documentation:
Returns new date object with the updated year.

Parameters:
    - date_obj: The date object.
    - to_year (int): The year to jump to.
    - leap_forward (bool): This is for cases where the `date_obj` given falls on February 29th and `to_year` is
    not a leap year. If set to `True` - will update date to March 1st; if `False` - will update date to February 28th.

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.update_month
documentation:
Returns new date object with the updated month.

Parameters:
    - date_obj: The date object.
    - to_month (int): The month to jump to.
    - jump_to_end (bool): Used for cases where the day of month is one of: [29, 30, 31]. If set to `True`, jumps
    to the last day of the month.

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.compute_absolute_date_difference
documentation: Computes the absolute date-difference, and returns a tuple of (years, days)

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.compute_date_difference
documentation: Computes the date-difference as `a - b`, and returns a tuple of (years, days)

functions.name: slupy.dates.utils.get_day_of_week
documentation:
Returns the day of the week.
Day of week options when `shorten` is set to False: ['Monday', 'Tuesday', 'Wednesday', 'Thursday', 'Friday', 'Saturday', 'Sunday'].
Day of week options when `shorten` is set to True: ['Mon', 'Tue', 'Wed', 'Thu', 'Fri', 'Sat', 'Sun'].

Used for cases where day-of-month could be 29, 30, 31.
Returns the day-of-month to use [1-31].

methods.name: slupy.dates.time_travel.TimeTravel.diff_for_humans
documentation:
Returns a string having a human-readable format of the difference between `value` and `initial_value`.
Computes it as `value` - `initial_value`.

Class that represents a time-traveller.

Instance methods:
    - add()
    - copy()
    - diff_for_humans()
    - subtract()

Properties:
    - dtype
    - initial_value
    - value

classes.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.__init__
documentation:
Parameters:
    - data (list): List of dictionaries.
    - deep_copy (bool): If `deep_copy=True`, creates a deep-copy of the given `data` and
    ensures that the original is never modified.
    - autofill (bool): If `autofill=True`, checks if the existing unique fields are present in each dictionary
    in the list. If not present, sets their default value to `None`. Does this operation inplace.

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.copy
documentation: Returns deep-copy of `self`

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.data_copy
documentation: Returns deep-copy of `self.data`

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.find_duplicate_indices
documentation:
Used to find the indices of the duplicate elements (if any).

Returns list of list of indices that correspond to duplicates. If no duplicates are found, returns an empty list.
Always returns non-negative indices. Each sub-list of indices will be sorted in ascending order.

Eg: An output of `[[0, 4, 5], [1, 6, 8]]` means that dictionaries at indices (0, 4, 5) are duplicates of the same
value; and dictionaries at indices (1, 6, 8) are duplicates of the same value; etc.

Parameters:
    - break_at (str): If `break_at='first'`, returns early with the first 2 indices of the first set of duplicates identified (if any).
    If `break_at='first_full'`, returns early with all the indices of the first set of duplicates identified (if any).
    - subset (List[str]): List of keys to consider in each dictionary in the list.

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset._is_equal
documentation: Compares the given 2 dictionaries based on the subset of keys

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.has_duplicates
documentation: Checks if the dataset has duplicate rows over the given `subset` of fields

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.drop_duplicates
documentation: Drops the duplicate rows

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.keep_duplicates
documentation: Keeps the duplicate rows

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.yield_values_by_field
documentation: Yields the values for the given field

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.get_values_by_field
documentation: Returns a list of values for the given field

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.get_datatypes_by_field
documentation: Returns dictionary having keys = fields, and values = set of all the unique types present in said field

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.get_unique_fields
documentation: Returns list of all the unique fields that are present (sorted in ascending order)

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.set_defaults_for_fields
documentation:
Checks if the given fields are present in each dictionary in the list.
If not present, sets their default value to `None`.

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.compute_field
documentation:
Applies the given function `func` to each dictionary in the list, and stores the result of `func` in the key `field` of each dictionary.
The `func` takes in the dictionary (row) as a parameter.

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.keep_fields
documentation: Keeps the given fields (if they exist)

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.drop_fields
documentation: Drops the given fields (if they exist)

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.reorder_fields
documentation: Re-orders the fields

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.fill_nulls
documentation: Fills all values that are `None` with `value`

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.autofill_missing_fields
documentation:
Checks if the existing unique fields are present in each dictionary in the list.
If not present, sets their default value to `None`.

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset._has_nulls
documentation: Checks if the given dictionary has value as `None` for any of the given `subset` of keys

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.drop_nulls
documentation: Drops rows having value as `None` in any of the given `subset` of fields

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.filter_rows
documentation:
Applies the given function `func` to each dictionary (row) in the list, and expects the `func` to return a boolean.
If the result is `True` then keeps the row; otherwise removes the row.
The `func` takes in the dictionary (row) as a parameter.

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.order_by
documentation: Orders by the given fields in the desired order. Returns a new instance having the ordered data.

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.rank_by
documentation:
Orders by the given fields in the desired order (as done in `Dataset.order_by()`), and then ranks them based on a given strategy (`rank_strategy`).
Adds a new field (`rank_field_name`) having the ranking.
Returns a new `Dataset` instance having the ranked data.

Example:
```
dataset.rank_by(
    fields=["text"],
    ascending=[False],
    rank_field_name="...",
    rank_strategy="...",
)

------------------------------------------------
row_number  |  rank  |  dense_rank  |  text
------------------------------------------------
    1       |  1     |  1           |  "BBB"
    2       |  1     |  1           |  "BBB"
    3       |  1     |  1           |  "BBB"
    4       |  1     |  1           |  "BBB"
    5       |  5     |  2           |  "AAA"
    6       |  5     |  2           |  "AAA"
    7       |  5     |  2           |  "AAA"
    8       |  8     |  3           |  None
    9       |  8     |  3           |  None
------------------------------------------------
```

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.concatenate
documentation: Concatenates the current dataset with the given datasets. The given `datasets` are never modified.

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.value_counts
documentation:
Returns dictionary having keys = fields, and values = `collections.Counter` objects having the value-counts
of all the values in said field.

methods.name: slupy.data_wrangler.dataset.Dataset.pretty_print
documentation: Pretty prints the value of `self.data`
Class that represents a dataset (collection of data as a list of dictionaries)

functions.name: slupy.dates.functions.offset_between_datetimes
documentation:
Returns list of datetime/date/string objects separated by the given offset.

For reference to `offset_kwargs` dictionary, please check the `slupy.dates.time_travel.TimeTravel` class' `add()`
and `subtract()` methods.

Examples:
\`
from datetime import date, datetime

```pycon
>>> offset_between_datetimes(
    start=date(year=2000, month=1, day=21),
    end=date(year=2000, month=1, day=27),
    offset_kwargs=dict(days=1),
    ascending=True,
    as_string=True,
)  # Returns ["2000-01-21", "2000-01-22", "2000-01-23", "2000-01-24", "2000-01-25", "2000-01-26", "2000-01-27"]
```

````pycon
>>> offset_between_datetimes(
    start=date(year=2000, month=1, day=21),
    end=date(year=2000, month=1, day=27),
    offset_kwargs=dict(days=1),
    ascending=False,
    as_string=True,
)  # Returns ["2000-01-27", "2000-01-26", "2000-01-25", "2000-01-24", "2000-01-23", "2000-01-22", "2000-01-21"]
```
````

functions.name: slupy.dates.functions.get_datetime_buckets
documentation:
Returns list of buckets of datetime/date/string objects, where each bucket is separated by the given offset.

For reference to `offset_kwargs` dictionary, please check the `slupy.dates.time_travel.TimeTravel` class' `add()`
and `subtract()` methods.

Examples:
\`
from datetime import date, datetime

```pycon
>>> get_datetime_buckets(
    start=date(year=2000, month=1, day=1),
    num_buckets=5,
    offset_kwargs=dict(weeks=1),
    ascending=True,
    as_string=True,
)  # Returns [("2000-01-01", "2000-01-07"), ("2000-01-08", "2000-01-14"), ("2000-01-15", "2000-01-21"), ("2000-01-22", "2000-01-28"), ("2000-01-29", "2000-02-04")]
```

````pycon
>>> get_datetime_buckets(
    start=date(year=2000, month=1, day=1),
    num_buckets=3,
    offset_kwargs=dict(weeks=1),
    ascending=False,
    as_string=True,
)  # Returns [("1999-12-12", "1999-12-18"), ("1999-12-19", "1999-12-25"), ("1999-12-26", "2000-01-01")]
```
````

functions.name: slupy.dates.functions.compute_date_difference_in_years
documentation: Computes the date-difference as `a - b`, and returns a float

functions.name: slupy.dates.functions.compute_date_difference_in_weeks_and_days
documentation: Computes the date-difference as `a - b`, and returns tuple of (weeks, days)

classes.name: slupy.dates.time_conversions.TimeUnitConverter
documentation: Class for time-related conversions

functions.name: slupy.email_ops.email_ops._add_attachments_to_multipart_object
documentation:
Adds attachments from the given filepaths to the given `MIMEMultipart` object.
Returns `MIMEMultipart` object containing the attachments.

Resources:
    - [Python email package examples](https://www.rose-hulman.edu/class/cs/archive/csse120-old/csse120-old-terms/201210/Resources/python-3.1.2-docs-html/library/email-examples.html)
    - [Common MIME types](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Basics_of_HTTP/MIME_types/Common_types)

functions.name: slupy.email_ops.email_ops.send_email
documentation:
Sends an email from one Email ID to one or more Email IDs, along with the attachments provided (if any).
Attachments work for the following file extensions: ['csv', 'docx', 'flv', 'jpg', 'm4a', 'mp3', 'mp4', 'pdf', 'png', 'txt', 'xls', 'xlsx', 'zip'].
Accepts HTML tags for the `body` parameter.
Returns None if the email is sent successfully; otherwise raises an Exception.

```pycon
>>> send_email(
        from_email_id='sender_email_id@gmail.com',
        from_email_id_password='some_password',
        to_email_ids=['person1@gmail.com', 'person2@gmail.com'],
        cc_email_ids=['person3@gmail.com'],
        subject="Your subject",
        body="Your message",
        filepaths_to_attachments=['file1.xlsx', 'file2.pdf'],
    )
```

functions.name: slupy.file_ops.file_ops.get_extension_from_filepath
documentation: Returns the extension of the given filepath

functions.name: slupy.file_ops.file_ops.get_basename_from_filepath
documentation: Returns base-name of the file/folder from the given `filepath` (along with the extension, if any)

functions.name: slupy.file_ops.file_ops.get_absolute_filepath
documentation: Returns absolute filepath of the file/folder from the given `filepath` (along with the extension, if any)

functions.name: slupy.file_ops.file_ops.filter_filepaths_by_extensions
documentation:
Filters given filepaths by the desired extensions.

```pycon
>>> filter_filepaths_by_extensions(
    filepaths=['one.js', 'two.py', 'three.css', 'four.go', 'five.html', 'six.py', 'seven.js'],
    extensions=['css', 'js'],
)
```

functions.name: slupy.file_ops.file_ops.get_unique_extensions
documentation: Returns all unique extensions available in the list of filepaths given

functions.name: slupy.file_ops.file_ops.get_filepaths
documentation:
Gets list of all filepaths (of files) from source directory.

Parameters:

: - src_dir (str): Filepath to the source directory. Can be absolute or relative.
  - depth (str): Options: ['all_levels', 'first_level'].

  Set to 'all_levels' if you want to get filepaths from all sub-directories (if any) in the given source directory.
  Set to 'first_level' if you want to get filepaths only from the first directory given.
  \- extensions (list): List of extensions to filter the filepaths by (optional).

```pycon
>>> get_filepaths(
        src_dir="SOME_SOURCE_DIR",
        depth='all_levels',
        extensions=['csv', 'xlsx'],
    )
```

functions.name: slupy.file_ops.file_ops.create_archive_file
documentation:
Creates archive file of the given source directory.
Options for `archive_format` are: ['zip', 'tar', 'gztar', 'bztar', 'xztar'].

Adds commas to number for better readability.

\>>> commafy_number(1738183090) # Returns "1,738,183,090"

` ``

functions.name: slupy.core.conversions.listify_string_of_nums

functions.name: slupy.core.checks.is_collection_of_items
documentation: If the given `obj` is one of `[list, tuple, set]`, returns `True`

functions.name: slupy.core.checks.is_list_of_instances_of_type
documentation: Returns True if `obj` is a list of instances of type `type_`

functions.name: slupy.core.checks.is_list_of_subclasses_of_type

documentation: Returns deep-copy of the given object

functions.name: slupy.core.helpers.make_shallow_copy

functions.name: slupy.core.helpers.slice_by_position
documentation:
Slice the given sliceable object by position (not by index).
The position can range from 1-n (where `n` is the length of the sliceable).

functions.name: slupy.core.helpers.compute_partitions
documentation:
Returns list of tuples having (start, end) indices.

Parameters:
    - length (int): Length of the data to be partitioned.
    - zero_based (bool): Indexing type of the partitions. If `zero_based=True`, will be 0-based; otherwise will be 1-based.
    - num_partitions (int): Number of partitions.
    - partition_size (int): Size of each partition.

functions.name: slupy.core.helpers.create_repr
documentation:
Returns a representation of a class' instance.

\`
class Person:

> pass

````pycon
>>> create_repr(
    instance=Person(),
    kwargs_dict={
        "first_name": "James",
        "last_name": "Murphy",
        "age": 35,
        "is_developer": True,
    },
    multi_line=False,
) # Returns the string: "Person(first_name='James', last_name='Murphy', age=35, is_developer=True)"
```
````

functions.name: slupy.core.helpers.print_docstring
documentation: Prints the doc-string (if available). Usually of a class, method or function.

functions.name: slupy.core.helpers.rename_dict_keys
documentation:
Renames the keys of the given dictionary, based on the given `update_func`.
Returns a new dictionary having the updated keys. The original dictionary will remain unchanged.

Parameters:
    - dict_: The dictionary.
    - update_func (Callable): The function that takes in the current key and returns the new key.
    - deep (bool): If `deep=True`, it also renames the dictionary keys of all the nested dictionaries.

functions.name: slupy.decorators.decorators.slow_down

documentation:

functions.name: slupy.strings.strings.to_dumbo_text
documentation:
Converts given text to retardified text.
\>>> to_dumbo_text("Hello, and good morning!") # Returns "hElLo, AnD gOoD mOrNiNg!"

functions.name: slupy.strings.strings.remove_characters_at_indices
documentation:
Removes characters present at the given `indices` in the `text`.
Expects `indices` to be in range (0, n-1) where n is the length of the `text`.
Raises an IndexError if any of the given `indices` are out of bounds.

functions.name: slupy.strings.strings.remove_characters_at_positions
documentation:
Removes characters present at the given `positions` in the `text`.
Expects `positions` to be in range (1, n) where n is the length of the `text`.
Raises an IndexError if any of the given `positions` are out of bounds.
